001/**
002 * Maintains the fixed vocabulary of primary commands understood by the {@link Parser}.
003 * <p>
004 * Each entry must stay synchronized with the branches handled in
005 * {@link GameEngine#interpretCommand(String)}.
006 * </p>
007 *
008 * @author Michael Kolling and David J. Barnes + D. Bureau
009 * @version 2008.03.30 + 2019.09.25
010 */
011public class CommandWords
012{
013    /** All valid first words, in display order for help text. */
014    private String[] aValidCommands = { "go", "help", "quit", "look", "cashin", "back", "test", "name", "take", "drop", "inventory" };
015    
016    /**
017     * Creates a CommandWords object containing all valid commands.
018     */
019    public CommandWords()
020    {
021    }
022    
023    /**
024     * Tests membership of {@code pString} in the internal command table (case-sensitive).
025     *
026     * @param pString candidate verb, usually lower-case
027     * @return {@code true} if {@code pString} is a known command word
028     */
029    public boolean isCommand( final String pString )
030    {
031        for ( int vI = 0; vI < this.aValidCommands.length; vI++ ) {
032            if ( this.aValidCommands[vI].equals( pString ) ) {
033                return true;
034            }
035        }
036        return false;
037    } // isCommand()
038
039    /**
040     * Builds a single string listing every valid command, separated by spaces.
041     *
042     * @return space-delimited command list for help output
043     */
044    public String getCommandList()
045    {
046        StringBuilder vChaineDesCommandes = new StringBuilder();
047        for ( String vCommand : this.aValidCommands ) {
048            vChaineDesCommandes.append( vCommand ).append( " " );
049        }
050        return vChaineDesCommandes.toString();
051    } // getCommandList()
052} // CommandWords
